tools.name: resolve-library-id
tools.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

tools.name: query-docs
tools.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Workflow: call first without researchMode. If that doesn't answer the question, retry with researchMode: true. Do not call each tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

researchMode.description: Retry the query with deep research: spins up sandboxed agents that read the actual source repos and runs a live web search, then synthesizes a fresh answer. Set true on retry if you weren't satisfied with the first answer and want a more thorough one. Requires an API key. You can get one free at https://context7.com.

tools.name: browser_close
tools.description: Close the page

tools.name: browser_resize
tools.description: Resize the browser window

tools.name: browser_console_messages
tools.description: Returns all console messages

level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

tools.name: browser_handle_dialog
tools.description: Handle a dialog

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

tools.name: browser_evaluate
tools.description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

function.description: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, result is returned as text.

tools.name: browser_file_upload
tools.description: Upload one or multiple files

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

tools.name: browser_drop
tools.description: Drop files or MIME-typed data onto an element, as if dragged from outside the page. At least one of "paths" or "data" must be provided.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

paths.description: Absolute paths to files to drop onto the element.

data.description: Data to drop, as a map of MIME type to string value (e.g. {"text/plain": "hello", "text/uri-list": "https://example.com"}).

tools.name: browser_fill_form
tools.description: Fill multiple form fields

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

value.description: Value to fill in the field. If the field is a checkbox, the value should be `true` or `false`. If the field is a combobox, the value should be the text of the option.

tools.name: browser_press_key
tools.description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into editable element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
tools.description: Go back to the previous page in the history

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: Returns a numbered list of network requests since loading the page. Use browser_network_request with the number to get full details.

static.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

filter.description: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

tools.name: browser_network_request
tools.description: Returns full details (headers and body) of a single network request, or a single part if `part` is set. Use the number from browser_network_requests.

index.description: 1-based index of the request, as printed by browser_network_requests.

part.description: Return only this part of the request. Omit to return full details.

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, output is returned as text.

tools.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
tools.description: Run a Playwright code snippet. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

filename.description: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

filename.description: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

depth.description: Limit the depth of the snapshot tree

boxes.description: Include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height] in the snapshot

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.description: Hover over element on page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_select_option
tools.description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

tools.name: browser_tabs
tools.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

url.description: URL to navigate to in the new tab, used for new.

tools.name: browser_wait_for
tools.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

tools.name: click
tools.description: Clicks on the provided element

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

dblClick.description: Set to true for double clicks. Default is false.

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: close_page
tools.description: Closes the page by its index. The last open page cannot be closed.

pageId.description: The ID of the page to close. Call list_pages to list pages.

tools.name: drag
tools.description: Drag an element onto another element

from_uid.description: The uid of the element to drag

to_uid.description: The uid of the element to drop into

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: emulate
tools.description: Emulates various features on the selected page.

networkConditions.description: Throttle network. Omit to disable throttling.

cpuThrottlingRate.description: Represents the CPU slowdown factor. Omit or set the rate to 1 to disable throttling

geolocation.description: Geolocation (`<latitude>x<longitude>`) to emulate. Latitude between -90 and 90. Longitude between -180 and 180. Omit to clear the geolocation override.

userAgent.description: User agent to emulate. Set to empty string to clear the user agent override.

colorScheme.description: Emulate the dark or the light mode. Set to "auto" to reset to the default.

viewport.description: Emulate device viewports '<width>x<height>x<devicePixelRatio>[,mobile][,touch][,landscape]'. 'touch' and 'mobile' to emulate mobile devices. 'landscape' to emulate landscape mode.

tools.name: evaluate_script
tools.description:
Evaluate a JavaScript function inside the currently selected page. Returns the response as JSON,
so returned values have to be JSON-serializable.

items.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

args.description: An optional list of arguments to pass to the function.

dialogAction.description: Handle dialogs while execution. "accept", "dismiss", or string for response of window.prompt. Defaults to accept.

tools.name: fill
tools.description: Type text into an input, text area or select an option from a <select> element.

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: fill_form
tools.description: Fill out multiple form elements at once

uid.description: The uid of the element to fill out

elements.description: Elements from snapshot to fill out.

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: get_console_message
tools.description: Gets a console message by its ID. You can get all messages by calling list_console_messages.

msgid.description: The msgid of a console message on the page from the listed console messages

tools.name: get_network_request
tools.description: Gets a network request by an optional reqid, if omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

reqid.description: The reqid of the network request. If omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

requestFilePath.description: The absolute or relative path to a .network-request file to save the request body to. If omitted, the body is returned inline.

responseFilePath.description: The absolute or relative path to a .network-response file to save the response body to. If omitted, the body is returned inline.

tools.name: handle_dialog
tools.description: If a browser dialog was opened, use this command to handle it

action.description: Whether to dismiss or accept the dialog

promptText.description: Optional prompt text to enter into the dialog.

tools.name: hover
tools.description: Hover over the provided element

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: lighthouse_audit
tools.description: Get Lighthouse score and reports for accessibility, SEO and best practices. This excludes performance. For performance audits, run performance_start_trace

outputDirPath.description: Directory for reports. If omitted, uses temporary files.

tools.name: list_console_messages
tools.description: List all console messages for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of messages to return. When omitted, returns all messages.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

types.description: Filter messages to only return messages of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all messages.

includePreservedMessages.description: Set to true to return the preserved messages over the last 3 navigations.

tools.name: list_network_requests
tools.description: List all requests for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of requests to return. When omitted, returns all requests.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

resourceTypes.description: Filter requests to only return requests of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all requests.

includePreservedRequests.description: Set to true to return the preserved requests over the last 3 navigations.

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: Get a list of pages open in the browser.

tools.name: navigate_page
tools.description: Go to a URL, or back, forward, or reload. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

type.description: Navigate the page by URL, back or forward in history, or reload.

ignoreCache.description: Whether to ignore cache on reload.

handleBeforeUnload.description: Whether to auto accept or beforeunload dialogs triggered by this navigation. Default is accept.

initScript.description: A JavaScript script to be executed on each new document before any other scripts for the next navigation.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tools.name: new_page
tools.description: Open a new tab and load a URL. Use project URL if not specified otherwise.

url.description: URL to load in a new page.

background.description: Whether to open the page in the background without bringing it to the front. Default is false (foreground).

isolatedContext.description: If specified, the page is created in an isolated browser context with the given name. Pages in the same browser context share cookies and storage. Pages in different browser contexts are fully isolated.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

tools.name: performance_analyze_insight
tools.description: Provides more detailed information on a specific Performance Insight of an insight set that was highlighted in the results of a trace recording.

insightSetId.description: The id for the specific insight set. Only use the ids given in the "Available insight sets" list.

insightName.description: The name of the Insight you want more information on. For example: "DocumentLatency" or "LCPBreakdown"

tools.name: performance_start_trace
tools.description: Start a performance trace on the selected webpage. Use to find frontend performance issues, Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS), and improve page load speed.

reload.description: Determines if, once tracing has started, the current selected page should be automatically reloaded. Navigate the page to the right URL using the navigate_page tool BEFORE starting the trace if reload or autoStop is set to true.

autoStop.description: Determines if the trace recording should be automatically stopped.

filePath.description: The absolute file path, or a file path relative to the current working directory, to save the raw trace data. For example, trace.json.gz (compressed) or trace.json (uncompressed).

tools.name: performance_stop_trace
tools.description: Stop the active performance trace recording on the selected webpage.

filePath.description: The absolute file path, or a file path relative to the current working directory, to save the raw trace data. For example, trace.json.gz (compressed) or trace.json (uncompressed).

tools.name: press_key
tools.description: Press a key or key combination. Use this when other input methods like fill() cannot be used (e.g., keyboard shortcuts, navigation keys, or special key combinations).

key.description: A key or a combination (e.g., "Enter", "Control+A", "Control++", "Control+Shift+R"). Modifiers: Control, Shift, Alt, Meta

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: resize_page
tools.description: Resizes the selected page's window so that the page has specified dimension

tools.name: select_page
tools.description: Select a page as a context for future tool calls.

pageId.description: The ID of the page to select. Call list_pages to get available pages.

bringToFront.description: Whether to focus the page and bring it to the top.

tools.name: take_memory_snapshot
tools.description: Capture a heap snapshot of the currently selected page. Use to analyze the memory distribution of JavaScript objects and debug memory leaks.

filePath.description: A path to a .heapsnapshot file to save the heapsnapshot to.

tools.name: take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the page or element.

format.description: Type of format to save the screenshot as. Default is "png"

quality.description: Compression quality for JPEG and WebP formats (0-100). Higher values mean better quality but larger file sizes. Ignored for PNG format.

uid.description: The uid of an element on the page from the page content snapshot. If omitted, takes a page screenshot.

fullPage.description: If set to true takes a screenshot of the full page instead of the currently visible viewport. Incompatible with uid.

filePath.description: The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the screenshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

tools.name: take_snapshot
tools.description:
Take a text snapshot of the currently selected page based on the a11y tree. The snapshot lists page elements along with a unique
identifier (uid). Always use the latest snapshot. Prefer taking a snapshot over taking a screenshot. The snapshot indicates the element selected
in the DevTools Elements panel (if any).

verbose.description: Whether to include all possible information available in the full a11y tree. Default is false.

filePath.description: The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the snapshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

tools.name: type_text
tools.description: Type text using keyboard into a previously focused input

submitKey.description: Optional key to press after typing. E.g., "Enter", "Tab", "Escape"

tools.name: upload_file
tools.description: Upload a file through a provided element.

uid.description: The uid of the file input element or an element that will open file chooser on the page from the page content snapshot

filePath.description: The local path of the file to upload

includeSnapshot.description: Whether to include a snapshot in the response. Default is false.

tools.name: wait_for
tools.description: Wait for the specified text to appear on the selected page.

text.description: Non-empty list of texts. Resolves when any value appears on the page.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.
